text:
Beginners Guide

On the screenshots, you will find translations. You can refer to the text below the picture for more information.

Login Screen

The login screen first appears when you open the app, and features an image of Chrollo, Uvogin, Kurapika, and Pakunoda. Upon opening the app, one of those characters will read out the name of the game. 

At the top of the screen, you can view your ID number, get help for various issues or contact the app's support, transfer your data, and view your achievements. 

You can tap on the screen to load the app and get taken to the home screen.

Team

On this screen, you can choose to create a team or join one.
 If you want to create a team, write the team's name in the first box.
 If you want to join one, put the team's ID in the second box.
 Be careful; the team will be disbanded if it's inactive for 120 days. If a team member is inactive for 14 days, they will be automatically kicked out.

Gacha

 Gacha banners listed on the gacha page will appear with the limited-time event banners first and the standard banners further back. There is a standard banner for every deck type in the game, except for the Free deck. The deck banners each have their own type of ticket that can be used to pull cards from them. Banners specifically for support cards or limited-time events will often have their own type of tickets as well. 

Individual deck banners will not appear on the gacha page if you do not currently possess any tickets usable for that deck banner; they will appear once you possess at least one of that ticket type, and disappear temporarily again when you no longer have any tickets in your inventory to spend on them. 

Other banners, like for limited-time events, will often allow you to use Jenny or Hunter Coins to make pulls, as well. 

You can check the card list and the card rates at the top. Most gacha banners will also allow you to select whether you want to do one or ten pulls. Some banners, however, like certain limited-time event banners or SS ticket banners, may only allow you to do either one pull or ten pulls, not both.

Bond Rewards

Leveling up your Bond character will provide certain rewards, namely Books, Nen stones, and Gacha tickets. 

However, certain levels will provide character-specific rewards. They are as follows: 

At Bond level. 10, you receive a coin sleeve designed with an icon related to that character 
At Bond level. 20, you receive a stamp featuring that character
At Bond level. 25, you receive the ability to customize your character's art with an animated effect related to them/their Nen ability.

Character Card Info

Character cards are among the three card types used in Arena Battle, the other two types being action cards and support cards. Cards are primarily obtained through the Gacha, but can also be obtained through events or by generating them with Nen Stones (念石, Nen Seki).

There are over 500 character cards, and they are ranked depending on their value. From lowest to highest, these ranks are B, A, S, and SS. These cards vary in attack power, abilities, combo skills, and GP (group point) costs.

Action Card Info

Action cards are among the three card types used in Arena Battle, the other two are character cards and support cards. Cards are primarily obtained through the Gacha, but can also be obtained through events or by generating them with Nen Stones (念石, Nen Seki).

There are over 200 action cards, and they are ranked depending on their value. From lowest to highest, these ranks are B, A, S, and SS. These cards vary in abilities and GP (group point) costs.

GP

Most cards have a GP (Group Point) cost associated with them (see the Character Card and Action Card sections for where to view a card's GP cost). This equates to how much GP you need to accumulate before you can successfully use a card. Remember that you can still play any character card you have not accumulated enough GP for, but the character card will do minimal damage and not be used for combos (it will still provide you with GP). Action cards, however, require their GP costs to be met in full before being played. 

A card's GP cost is sorted into two groups: a GP cost associated with a specific deck and a general GP cost. The GP cost with color is associated with the card's deck and denotes how many cards from that deck type must be played before the card becomes usable. GP costs not associated with any deck will have no deck color associated with it (displayed as a light grey). Some cards have both GP deck costs and a standard GP cost; some have just one or the other; some have none.

There are other ways to gain GP besides playing character cards on the board, namely, using certain action cards. 

Card Groups

All cards fall under one of the eight deck groups listed below.

Free (フリー, Furī)

The Free deck contains cards with various characters that may not specifically fall under any of the other groups. Characters listed in the Free group will not have a GP cost associated with any of the other decks in order to play, and therefore also don't give GP specific to any other deck. 

Most often, cards from the Free group fill spaces within the other deck types, especially while creating a deck in Select matches. However, making an entire deck out of the Free cards is possible. 

287th Exam Applicants (287期受験生, 287ki Jukensei)

The 287th Exam Applicants deck revolves around the exam, or "tests", so it specializes in cards with conditions to activate their skills.

Phantom Troupe (幻影旅団, Gen'ei Ryodan)

The Phantom Troupe deck revolves around the bond between the Spiders and therefore specializes in strong cards with high Group Point costs.

Mafia Community (マフィアンコミュニティー, Mafian Komyunitī)

The Mafia Community deck primarily revolves around Kurapika and the Nostrade Family, but also includes other Mafia groups and characters that made appearances in the Yorknew City arc, such as the Shadow Beasts and Freelance Assassins. The deck's specialty is "vows and limitations," with most card abilities requiring the user's HP to be below 60 in order to activate.

Although all of Neon's bodyguards, except for Tocino, have SS rank character cards in the deck, the majority of the high-level deck cards are of Kurapika and Neon, where Neon uses her fortune-telling abilities to predict the next card the player will pull, among other things, and where Kurapika's primarily focus on his different chain abilities for a variety of uses. 

Greed Island Players (G.I.プレイヤー, Jī-Ai Pureiyā)

The Greed Island deck primarily revolves around using cards, specifically the act. Most card abilities require a certain number of action cards to be used to activate. The deck also includes the Bomber trio, which would create a deck that specializes in planting numerous bombs across the board so they detonate before the opponent can disarm them.

Chimera Ants (キメラアント, Kimera Anto)

Many Chimera Ant cards use the "greed" ability, which consumes your own character cards on the board to activate their skills. Some cards can consume your opponent's character cards on the board. 

This deck also contains cards with skills that activate when your opponent uses character cards with a high ATK, so it is best when used against decks like 287th Exam Applicants or Ant Extermination Team. This deck also has cards that raise the ATK of your opponent's character cards to make it easier to activate those skills.

Ant Extermination Team (蟻と戦う者, Ari to Tatakau Monoーlit. "Those Who Fight Ants")

Many Ant Extermination Team cards have the "competition" ability and will rely on you having character cards with a high ATK in your hand or on the board to activate your skills. Similar to the 287th Exam Applicants deck, this deck will have cards that raise the ATK of your character cards to make activating those skills easier. 

Zoldyck Family (ゾルディック家, Zorudikku-ke)

Many of the Zoldyck Family cards use the abilities "haste" and/or "stealth" or work in tandem with cards from the deck with those abilities. Many higher-level cards will also use the "penetrate" ability, allowing them to bypass any reduction or counter that the opponent has set up when doing damage. 

Most of Illumi's character and action cards in the Zoldyck Family deck are used to create and utilize needle people from the player's empty tokens on the board. 

In addition, many of the cards in the Zoldyck Family deck require a sacrifice of sorts, usually by choosing a card to draw into the player's hand after discarding another from the deck, discarding a card when placing a character card on the board, or discarding a card to increase a card's attack. 

Types Of Abilities, Combos, And Effects

{| class="article-table" border="0" cellspacing="1" cellpadding="1" style="width:100%;"
|-
! scope="col" style="text-align:center;" |Type
! scope="col" style="text-align:center;" |Effect
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Atk Up

| style="text-align:center;" |ATK Up (攻撃力アップ, Kougekiryoku Appuーlit. Attack Power Up) Raises the ATK of a card
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Atk Down

| style="text-align:center;" |ATK Down (攻撃力アップ, Kougekiryoku Daunーlit. Attack Power Down) Reduces the ATK of a card
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Special Damage

| style="text-align:center;" |Special Damage (特殊ダメージ, Tokushu Damēji) Deals "Special" damage
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Nen Damage

| style="text-align:center;" |Nen Damage (念ダメージ, Nen Damēji) Deals "Nen" damage
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Recovery

| style="text-align:center;" |Recovery (回復, Kaifuku) Recovers HP
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Defense

| style="text-align:center;" |Defense (防御, Bōgyo) Reduces damage taken
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Summon

| style="text-align:center;" |Summon (召喚, Shōkan) Turns an empty card on the board into a character
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Card Management

| style="text-align:center;" |Card Management (カード操作, Kādo Sōsa) Controls cards in your hand or deck. There are four types of effects: discard from your hand, return a card to your deck, move cards in your deck and draw a card
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Card Generation

| style="text-align:center;" |Card Generation (カード生成, Kādo Seisei) Generates a new card
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Card Destruction

| style="text-align:center;" |Card Destruction (カード破壊, Kādo Hakai) Discards a card from your opponent's hand or turns one of his cards into an empty card
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Card Lock

| style="text-align:center;" |Card Lock (カードロック, Kādo Rokku) Temporarily disables a card in opponent's hand
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Gp Management

| style="text-align:center;" |GP Management (GP操作, Jīpī Sōsa) Increases your GP
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Self Harm

| style="text-align:center;" |Self Damage (自傷, Jishōーlit. Self-Harm) Deals special damage to yourself
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Damage Up

| style="text-align:center;" |Damage Up (ダメージアップ, Damēji' Appu) Increases normal, Special or Nen damage
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Ability Erasure

| style="text-align:center;" |Ability Erasure (能力消去, Nōryoku Shōkyo) Erases a character card's ability and combo
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Unique Ability

| style="text-align:center;" |Unique Ability (ユニーク能力, Yunīku Nōryoku) Doesn't belong to any other type and is specific to the character
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Counter

| style="text-align:center;" |Counter (カウンター, Kauntā) When the damage specified on the card is received, it counters by returning or reducing the damage received
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Timer

| style="text-align:center;" |Timer The card ability or effect will activate after the specified amount of turns
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |View

| style="text-align:center;" |View See cards in the opponent's hand
|-
! scope="row" style="text-align:center;" |Time Up

| style="text-align:center;" |Time Up Speeds up abilities on cards with the Timer effect
|}
